expectations.text: Identifies apparent violation of Entity List restriction (§744.11 or Entity List controls)
expectations.evidence: Part 1 explicitly identifies the violation: 'constitutes an apparent unauthorized reexport under 15 CFR § 736.2(b)(1)' and 'The Entity List imposes a license requirement for ALL items subject to the EAR... when shipped to a listed party (15 CFR § 744.11).'

expectations.text: Advises filing Voluntary Self-Disclosure (VSD) with BIS/OEE
expectations.evidence: Part 3 is entirely dedicated to 'THE VOLUNTARY SELF-DISCLOSURE (VSD) PROCESS UNDER § 764.5' and Part 2, Step 5 states 'File an Initial VSD Notification to Preserve the VSD Date' as an immediate action.

expectations.text: Explains VSD is a mitigating factor in penalty calculation
expectations.evidence: Part 4.2 states 'BIS's penalty mitigation guidelines expressly identify a VSD as a strong mitigating factor' and describes outcomes 'WITH a VSD + strong ECP + full cooperation + remediation' including '50% or more reduction from base penalty.'

expectations.text: Mentions civil penalty exposure (dollar amount or 2× transaction value)
expectations.evidence: Part 4.1 states 'Maximum civil penalty: Greater of $374,474 per violation (FY2025 inflation-adjusted figure) OR 2 times the value of the transaction.'

expectations.text: Recommends immediate steps: stop transactions, preserve records, escalate to counsel
expectations.evidence: Part 2 covers immediate steps including Step 1 'Convene an Internal Crisis Response Team' (escalate to counsel), Step 2 'Preserve All Records — Litigation Hold', and Step 3 'Stop Any Related Transactions — Immediately place the Singapore customer on hold for all pending orders.'
